text:
JYP Entertainment is hiring for AI and Unreal Engine experts to develop an ‚Äėunprecedented‚Äô virtual K-pop artist
September 3, 2025
By
Mandy Dalugdug
Credit: Stock-Asso/Shutterstock
It‚Äôs a powerhouse of K-Pop, generating
hundreds of millions of dollars
in revenue each year.
But Seoul-based JYP Entertainment‚Äôs next global superstar might well be less human than their predecessors.
JYP, currently riding high on the success of
Stray Kids
, is hiring staff with expertise in artificial intelligence, motion graphics, Unreal Engine, and more as it prepares to develop an ‚Äú
unprecedented
‚ÄĚ AI artist through its tech business unit,
Blue Garage
.
Job ads listed on the company‚Äôs website indicate that JYP is recruiting for multiple roles for its AI Artist project, from Creative Production and Product Design execs to
LLM Engineers
.
It is also hiring for√ā
positions such as
√ā¬†3D Art Director
,√ā
Character Interaction Engineer,√ā
and√ā

Epic Games,

JYP‚Äôs move comes as virtual performers gain momentum both in South Korea and in the wider
global entertainment industry
.
JYP rival
HYBE
introduced
virtual pop group
SYNDI8
in July 2024, with their voices developed using AI technology from
Supertone
, which is majority-owned by HYBE, after it struck

Separately, SM
Entertainment
debuted
a virtual artist called naevis last year.
The news also comes hot on the heels of the global rise of a fictional K-pop girl group called

from√ā¬†animated movie

most watched

HUNTR/X√Ę¬Ä¬ôs
Golden,
taken from the movie‚Äôs soundtrack, has just
topped the US Hot 100
for a third week, with four tracks from the film in total present in the US Top 10. The soundtrack also continues to dominate
Spotify
charts, with the Top 5 of the platform‚Äôs US Daily and Weekly chart currently occupied by music from the film.
JYP‚Äôs virtual artist bet arrives amid significant global success with one of its flagship groups,
Stray
√ā
Kids.

latest album
KARMA
just debuted at

200,
with the group√ā¬†claimed to be the first act in history to have their first seven Billboard 200 entries debut at No.1.
They are also claimed to have√ā¬†broken the record for√ā¬†the most No.1 albums on the Billboard 200 by any K-Pop act of all time.

ublic Records,

and Belgium

in Australia.
In√ā
terms of the K-pop company‚Äôs AI artist ambitions, JYP said in a statement cited by Korean media that it wants to answer the following question
: ‚ÄúCan AI become a being that calls fans‚Äô names and interacts with them?‚ÄĚ
